_comment: SLICE_S3_CONTRACT.md §4/§5 — additive manifest, union-merge-friendly with backend/domain-core/Svac.DomainCore/Config/manifests/domain-core.config.json. Signup/auth/me keys from the earlier passes (session.*, email_code.*, email.send_daily_cap, handle.cooldown_days, signup.verified_token_ttl_minutes, email_challenge.retention_hours, handle_history.retention_months, device.register_daily_cap) PLUS the export pass's three keys (export.link_ttl_hours, export.statutory_deadline_days, export.daily_cap) — every key here has a live consumer named in its own `consumer` field (the dead-tunable lint, §4). deletion.* keys (grace_days, retirement_days, deletion.*) remain Pass 2b's, deliberately absent — the deletion pipeline/worker is explicitly out of THIS pass's scope. The quota.*.cap rows beyond the literal §4 table are the QuotaService.Consume fixed naming convention (`quota.<quotaKey>.cap`, Svac.DomainCore/Quota/QuotaService.cs:24) — §5's quota keys (identity.email.send.daily, identity.device.register.daily, identity.export.request.daily) cannot resolve a cap without a row under exactly that name; identity.email.send_daily_cap/identity.device.register_daily_cap/identity.export.daily_cap are the contract's own literal key names, kept as the human-facing/desk-facing entries (the quota.*.cap pair is what QuotaService actually reads).

entries.consumer: Svac.Identity session issuance (signup/complete, auth/session, auth/refresh) — access token TTL
Svac.Identity refresh-token minting — refresh token TTL
Svac.Identity session issuance — active-session cap (oldest revoked past cap, reason cap_evicted)
Svac.Identity email-challenge confirm — brute-force lockout per row
identity.email.send.daily quota cap (desk-facing mirror of quota.identity.email.send.daily.cap below)
Svac.Identity handle-change — cooldown check (identity-local, NOT a 10A key, §5)
13A retention_expiry sweep of identity.email_challenges (sweep worker; Pass 1 ships the config + registered retention rule, the sweep loop lands with the first 13A worker)
post-deletion identity.handle_history sweep (impersonation-defense window; Pass 2's deletion worker)
identity.device.register.daily quota cap (desk-facing mirror of quota.identity.device.register.daily.cap below)
Svac.DomainCore.Quota.QuotaService.Consume("identity.email.send.daily") — the fixed quota.<key>.cap convention; mirrors identity.email.send_daily_cap
Svac.DomainCore.Quota.QuotaService.Consume("identity.device.register.daily") — the fixed quota.<key>.cap convention; mirrors identity.device.register_daily_cap
Svac.Identity.Export.ExportWorker — the Art. 12(3) statutory clock, written onto every export's manifest.json as statutoryDeadlineAt (the S5 desk's future render target)
identity.export.request.daily quota cap (desk-facing mirror of quota.identity.export.request.daily.cap below); floor 1 — no ops edit can zero a legal right
Svac.DomainCore.Quota.QuotaService.Consume("identity.export.request.daily") — the fixed quota.<key>.cap convention; mirrors identity.export.daily_cap
